name: Data from Cloud Storage
description: Adversaries access data stored in Azure Blob Storage, Azure Data Lake Storage, Azure Files, or SharePoint Online to collect sensitive information. In Azure, access to storage is logged in StorageBlobLogs and AzureActivity. Adversaries may use a compromised storage account key, SAS token, OAuth token (storage access via Graph or Azure Storage REST API), or misconfigured public access to download blobs. SharePoint and OneDrive access is logged in OfficeActivity and CloudAppEvents.
iocs: StorageBlobLogs with OperationName 'GetBlob' showing high-volume downloads from a single CallerIPAddress in a short window
StorageBlobLogs AuthenticationType == 'SAS' with a SAS token used from an IP not matching where the token was generated
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/LISTKEYS/ACTION' — storage key listing, often precedes programmatic bulk download
StorageBlobLogs bulk access to a container with ResponseBodySize totaling gigabytes within a single hour
OfficeActivity Operation 'FileDownloaded' or 'FileSyncDownloadedFull' from SharePoint by a user with abnormally high volume vs baseline
StorageBlobLogs OperationName 'ListBlobs' followed immediately by sequential 'GetBlob' operations — automated enumeration and download pattern
AzureActivity showing a new SAS token policy created on a storage account with ReadOnly permissions — attacker staging data access
evasion_variants: Use a pre-existing SAS token with full permissions to avoid generating a new LISTKEYS event
Download blobs in small batches spread across multiple hours to stay under volume-based thresholds
Access public containers that require no authentication — no auth logs generated in StorageBlobLogs
Use Azure Data Factory or Logic Apps with a Managed Identity to access storage — activity attributed to the service resource rather than an individual
Download only targeted blobs matching naming conventions for sensitive data (backup, key, secret, credential) rather than bulk downloading
Use Azcopy with a SAS token from a legitimate-looking IP (Azure region IP of the victim organization) to blend in with expected cloud-to-cloud transfers
Access storage via an Azure Private Endpoint from a compromised internal VM — no external IP in StorageBlobLogs CallerIPAddress
detection_kql_hints: StorageBlobLogs | where OperationName == 'GetBlob' | summarize TotalBytes = sum(ResponseBodySize), RequestCount = count() by CallerIpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h) | where TotalBytes > 1073741824
AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue == 'MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/LISTKEYS/ACTION' | project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, ResourceGroup, Resource

StorageBlobLogs | where OperationName == 'ListBlobs' | join kind=inner (StorageBlobLogs | where OperationName == 'GetBlob') on AccountName, CallerIpAddress | where abs(datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, TimeGenerated1)) < 5
StorageBlobLogs | where AuthenticationType == 'SAS' | extend SasExpiry = tostring(parse_json(RequestParameters).sasExpiry) | summarize count() by CallerIpAddress, SasExpiry | where count_ > 100
OfficeActivity | where Operation in ('FileDownloaded','FileSyncDownloadedFull') | summarize DailyDownloads=count() by UserId, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d) | where DailyDownloads > 500
